title: Printer's Triple Console Cabinet
summary: A long three-bay console with deep drawers flanking a pair of doors, faced in printer's-style plank fronts. Big, boxy, a lot of cabinetry.
description:
This is a statement console — six feet of low cabinet with three bays: deep drawers on the outsides and doors in the middle, all wearing planked "printer's cabinet" faces made of stacked 1x boards. It's a real casework project, and honest about it: face frames front and back, a plywood box, drawers on euro slides, and a lot of repetitive face-board work.

The assembly order the source insists on is worth following. Build the door and drawer faces while the face frame is still loose and flat on the bench — lining up eleven narrow boards and keeping them square is far easier against a frame you can move than against an assembled cabinet. And as always with drawers, build the boxes to your actual finished openings and slides, not to the drawing; the given numbers assume perfect stock and 1/2"-per-side slides.

The plywood gets ripped into 15-3/4" strips (the plan calls them "1x16s"). Grab the extra sheet if you want interior shelves — deciding after the box is built means a second trip and a second setup.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood into 15-3/4" strips accurately.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood
materials.note: Main box, ripped into 15-3/4" strips. Add a second sheet if you want shelves.

cutList.note: Back face frame omits the middle drawer dividers.
Cut to fit your openings and slides.

steps.title: Rip the plywood
steps.body: Have all the plywood ripped into 15-3/4" wide strips (the plan calls these "1x16s"). If you want interior shelves, get the extra sheet now and rip it too — deciding later means another setup.

steps.title: Build the two face frames
steps.body: Build the front and back face frames from 2x2. Note the back frame omits the middle drawer dividers. Build them flat and square.

steps.title: Build the door and drawer faces against the frame
steps.body: Build the planked door and drawer faces now, while the frame is loose — use the frame to align them. Getting eleven narrow boards square on an assembled cabinet is a fight; on a flat, movable frame it's easy.

steps.title: Assemble the box
steps.body: Connect the two face frames with the top and bottom, using glue and screws, and check for square. Add the sides, checking square again. Then add the dividers, keeping them flush to the inside of each compartment.

steps.title: Add slide supports and build drawers
steps.body: Add the drawer-slide supports to the outer compartments only (the middle bay has doors, not drawers). Then build three drawers to suit your finished openings and slides — the given cuts assume perfect stock and 1/2"-per-side slides.

steps.title: Install drawers, faces, doors, and top
steps.body: Install the drawers inset 3/4" at the front to leave room for the faces. Attach the drawer faces with an even 1/8" gap all around, hang the doors on the inset hinges, and add the top. Add all the cup handles, then finish.

images.alt: A long three-bay console cabinet with drawers and doors faced in planked printer's-style fronts
